title: Huperzia × josephbeitelii
intro: Huperzia × josephbeitelii, also known as a hybrid species of the Huperzia genus, is a type of liverwort, a small, non-vascular plant that belongs to the moss family. The health benefits of Huperzia × josephbeitelii include its potential to enhance cognitive function and memory, as well as its possible neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory properties, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. The bioactive constituents of this plant include huperzine A, a potent inhibitor of acetylcholinesterase, an enzyme that breaks down the neurotransmitter acetylcholine, as well as other alkaloids such as huperzine B and huperzine C. Huperzia × josephbeitelii is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as capsules, tablets, and extracts, which are used to support brain health and cognitive function.
